quotes.quote: The LLM produces Python code, which is then executed and scored on a sandbox. Searching over strategies dramatically increases performance: The system uses the score together with output logs and other information to hill climb towards a better score.
quotes.rationale: Sandbox execution produces scores and logs that are routed back into candidate improvement, i.e. implemented computational feedback.

quotes.quote: A scorable task, together with research ideas proposing methods to solve the task, are fed to an LLM, which produces code to evaluate the scorable task in a sandbox. This is then embedded within a tree search algorithm, whereby new nodes are chosen balancing exploitation and exploration, sampling from the LLM (Methods).
quotes.rationale: Confirms the scoring of code in a sandbox is embedded within the search loop.

quotes.quote: The node with the globally maximum PUCT𝑖 score is selected. The language model then generates a single novel child solution conditioned on this parent’s code and score. The new code is executed, assigned an empirical score, and appended to the search tree, followed by a backpropagation of the visit count to its ancestors. This global, flat-tree structure allows the system to seamlessly backtrack and branch from any historical node if the current optimization path plateaus.
quotes.rationale: A deterministic PUCT rule uses empirical scores to control which node is expanded and appends results to the tree.

quotes.quote: approach does not rely on beam search (which aggressively prunes unselected candidates at each depth layer) nor a (1 + 𝜆 ) evolution strategy (which maintains only the most recent generation and discards historical states).
quotes.rationale: Specifies that candidates are not pruned and historical states are retained, characterizing the search-control memory.

quotes.quote: Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).
quotes.rationale: Human experts judge post hoc whether code adheres to the requested algorithms; no routing back into the search is reported.

quotes.quote: Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).
quotes.rationale: Consistency of performance across replicate runs is reported as a distinct reproducibility assessment alongside the compliance inspection.

diagnostics.consequence: Hill climbing may optimize a metric whose relation to genuine task quality is unestablished, so reported gains cannot be separated from score exploitation.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The LLM produces Python code, which is then executed and scored on a sandbox. Searching over strategies dramatically increases performance: The system uses the score together with output logs and other information to hill climb towards a better score.

diagnostics.statement: No reliability study is reported for the sandbox score that drives search control; the score functions as an unvalidated proxy for solution quality.

diagnostics.consequence: The central specification-compliance claim rests on an unaudited internal judgment that readers cannot check or reproduce.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).

diagnostics.statement: The expert manual inspection of algorithmic adherence is reported only as a summary conclusion, with no rater count, blinding, rubric, or agreement statistic, and is internal to the reporting team.

diagnostics.consequence: The degree of autonomy versus human curation of tasks, research ideas, and search settings cannot be determined, limiting comparability with other systems.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: A scorable task, together with research ideas proposing methods to solve the task, are fed to an LLM, which produces code to evaluate the scorable task in a sandbox. This is then embedded within a tree search algorithm, whereby new nodes are chosen balancing exploitation and exploration, sampling from the LLM (Methods).

The node with the globally maximum PUCT𝑖 score is selected. The language model then generates a single novel child solution conditioned on this parent’s code and score. The new code is executed, assigned an empirical score, and appended to the search tree, followed by a backpropagation of the visit count to its ancestors. This global, flat-tree structure allows the system to seamlessly backtrack and branch from any historical node if the current optimization path plateaus.

diagnostics.statement: Human authority over generation, execution, memory, and policy configuration is not stated in the packet; only a validator role is observable through the manual inspection.

diagnostics.consequence: Any claim that the system produces research contributions rather than benchmark optimization is unsupported by the validator channels reported here.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The LLM produces Python code, which is then executed and scored on a sandbox. Searching over strategies dramatically increases performance: The system uses the score together with output logs and other information to hill climb towards a better score.

Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).

diagnostics.statement: No channel in the packet evaluates novelty or significance; implemented validators target only executability, benchmark score, algorithmic compliance, and run-to-run consistency.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The LLM produces Python code, which is then executed and scored on a sandbox. Searching over strategies dramatically increases performance: The system uses the score together with output logs and other information to hill climb towards a better score.

recommendations.proposed_action: Report a property-specific validation of the sandbox score that drives search control, comparing in-search scores of selected solutions against an independently computed held-out evaluation to test whether score gains track genuine task quality.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Reported quantitative agreement on a held-out evaluation, with any systematic inflation of in-search scores stated explicitly
success_check.evidence_needed: A published table or figure pairing in-search and independent scores for the selected solutions
success_check.observable: Correspondence between in-search sandbox score and an independently computed evaluation of the same solutions

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).

recommendations.proposed_action: Publish the manual code-inspection protocol, including number of independent raters, blinding status, rubric, and an inter-rater agreement statistic for the algorithm-adherence judgment.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: At least two independent raters with a reported agreement coefficient and per-item ratings released
success_check.evidence_needed: Methods text and a released per-solution rating sheet
success_check.observable: Documented inspection protocol plus an agreement statistic across independent raters

exact_quotes.exact_quote: A scorable task, together with research ideas proposing methods to solve the task, are fed to an LLM, which produces code to evaluate the scorable task in a sandbox. This is then embedded within a tree search algorithm, whereby new nodes are chosen balancing exploitation and exploration, sampling from the LLM (Methods).

The node with the globally maximum PUCT𝑖 score is selected. The language model then generates a single novel child solution conditioned on this parent’s code and score. The new code is executed, assigned an empirical score, and appended to the search tree, followed by a backpropagation of the visit count to its ancestors. This global, flat-tree structure allows the system to seamlessly backtrack and branch from any historical node if the current optimization path plateaus.

Importantly, expert manual inspection of the code solutions proposed by ERA confirmed that nearly all implementations adhered to the requested algorithms (Extended Data Table 1), with performance largely consistent across replicate runs of methods (Extended Data Fig. 1).

recommendations.proposed_action: State explicitly, per pipeline stage, which human roles supply scorable tasks and research ideas, configure search hyperparameters, curate the tree memory, and approve outputs, and state what property beyond task score is judged.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Every stage labeled with a concrete human role or an explicit statement of no human involvement, and each validator labeled with its target property
success_check.evidence_needed: A methods subsection or table enumerating human roles and validator targets across the pipeline
success_check.observable: An explicit per-stage statement of human involvement and of the properties each validator judges

memory_structure: global flat search tree of executed nodes with empirical scores and backpropagated visit counts
retained historical states with no pruning of unselected candidates
parent code and score used as conditioning context for child generation
policy_type: PUCT-based tree search balancing exploitation and exploration
score-driven hill climbing over sandbox execution results and logs
backtracking and branching from any historical node when the optimization path plateaus
